chats.noMessagesInChat: No messages yet. Send the first message to get started!
chats.noPermission: You do not have permission to send messages
chats.placeholderDesc: Select an active chat from the left sidebar to start reading and sending WhatsApp messages.

errors.markRead: Failed to mark chat as read

create.title: Create New Session

create.hint: Use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only. Example: customer-support, bot-1
create.invalidChars: Invalid characters. Only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens allowed.
create.tooLong: Name must be 50 characters or less ({{length}}/50).
create.duplicate: Session with this name already exists.

delete.title: Confirm Delete
delete.message: Are you sure you want to delete session "{{name}}"?

qr.title: Scan QR Code
qr.step3: 3. Tap Link a Device and scan this QR
qr.unavailable: QR code not available yet. Try again in a moment.

toasts.failedDesc: The WhatsApp session failed to start. Open the session to see the reason.

webhooks.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this webhook?

templates.title: Templates
templates.subtitle: Create reusable message templates for each WhatsApp session
templates.previewEmpty: Start writing a template to preview it here.
templates.deleteConfirm: Delete template "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.

empty.title: No templates saved
empty.description: Create a reusable template to speed up common replies and notifications.
empty.noSessionsDesc: Create a WhatsApp session before adding templates.

apiKeys.createdHint: Copy this key now. You won't be able to see it again.

confirm.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to permanently delete {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.
confirm.revokeMessage: Are you sure you want to revoke {{name}}? It will no longer work.

empty.title: No logs found
empty.description: Audit logs will appear here as actions are performed

messageTester.selectGroupHint: Select a group from your WhatsApp
messageTester.caption: Caption
messageTester.responseEmpty: Send a message to see the API response

phoneNumberGenerator.title: Number Generator
phoneNumberGenerator.subtitle: Generate realistic random phone numbers across multiple countries for data analysis and testing
phoneNumberGenerator.searchCountries: Search by name, code, or dial code...
phoneNumberGenerator.noCountriesSelected: Select at least one country to generate numbers
phoneNumberGenerator.emptyDesc: Select countries, set your quantity, and click Generate to create a dataset for analysis.
phoneNumberGenerator.connectSessionHint: Connect a WhatsApp session from Sessions to verify numbers and send group messages.
phoneNumberGenerator.noSession: Select a connected WhatsApp session first
phoneNumberGenerator.validatedVaultDesc: Verified numbers are saved to the server database and grouped by country — they persist across browsers and sessions
phoneNumberGenerator.vaultEmpty: Verified numbers will appear here organized by country after WhatsApp verification
phoneNumberGenerator.groupBroadcastDesc: Automatically create one WhatsApp group per country folder and send your research message
phoneNumberGenerator.groupMessagePlaceholder: Enter your research message to broadcast to each country group...
phoneNumberGenerator.noValidatedInVault: No validated numbers in vault — verify numbers first
phoneNumberGenerator.researchNote: Research tool: creates one group per country with validated numbers (max 256 per group). Use responsibly and comply with WhatsApp terms and local regulations.
phoneNumberGenerator.throttleSettingsDesc: Control batch size and delays to avoid WhatsApp rate limits during number checks
phoneNumberGenerator.batchSizeHint: Numbers checked per batch before a longer pause
phoneNumberGenerator.batchPauseHint: Rest period after each batch completes
phoneNumberGenerator.autoGroupPermissionDesc: Grant permission for the system to create WhatsApp groups from validated vault folders and send your research message. This action uses your connected session.
phoneNumberGenerator.groupPermissionRequired: Enable automatic group creation permission first
phoneNumberGenerator.groupCreateDelayHint: Throttle pause between creating each country group
phoneNumberGenerator.directBroadcastDesc: Send your message to each validated contact one-by-one with throttling. Progress is saved so you can resume after a network drop or session reconnect.
phoneNumberGenerator.directMessagePlaceholder: Enter the message to send individually to each validated contact...
phoneNumberGenerator.broadcastMessageDelayHint: Pause between each direct message (recommended 3–10 seconds)
phoneNumberGenerator.broadcastResumeDesc: {{pending}} of {{total}} contacts remaining ({{sent}} already sent). Reconnect your session and resume to continue with the same numbers.
phoneNumberGenerator.broadcastCancelledDesc: {{sent}} sent · {{pending}} still pending — use Resume after reconnect
phoneNumberGenerator.directBroadcastNote: Messages are sent one contact at a time. If WhatsApp disconnects, restart the session and click Resume to continue with the same numbers.
phoneNumberGenerator.broadcastPermissionTitle: Allow sending messages to validated contacts
phoneNumberGenerator.broadcastPermissionDesc: Grant permission to send direct messages using your connected WhatsApp session. Required for throttled broadcast and group creation.
phoneNumberGenerator.sessionNotRecovered: WhatsApp session did not reconnect in time
phoneNumberGenerator.automationDesc: Generate numbers, verify on WhatsApp, save contacts, and send template messages — one click with human-like stealth pacing.
phoneNumberGenerator.automationTemplateHint: Pair templates to regions on the Templates page. Use {{countryName}}, {{e164}}, {{phone}} placeholders.
phoneNumberGenerator.stealthModeDesc: Small batches, random delays between checks and messages — mimics normal human usage to reduce ban risk.
phoneNumberGenerator.automationVolumeWarningDesc: Generating {{count}} numbers per country is aggressive. For stealth experiments, keep at or below {{recommended}} per country.
phoneNumberGenerator.scopeCustomHint: Pick countries manually in the sidebar, then run the pipeline.

infrastructure.title: Infrastructure
infrastructure.subtitle: Configure server, database, cache, storage, and engine settings

database.title: Database Configuration
database.builtInDesc: OpenWA will manage a PostgreSQL container for you
database.sslDesc: Enable TLS/SSL for secure database connections
database.sslRejectUnauthorizedDesc: Reject self-signed or invalid certificates. Turn off for managed Postgres with self-signed certs (Supabase, Heroku, Render, Railway).
database.migrationsHint: Migrations are managed automatically. Use CLI for manual migrations.

redis.title: Redis
redis.enableDesc: Required for caching, session storage, and BullMQ queues
redis.builtInDesc: OpenWA will manage a Redis container for you
redis.queueDesc: Use message queues for reliable message and webhook delivery
redis.disabledDesc: Enable Redis for caching, session storage, and BullMQ message queues.

storage.title: Storage Configuration
storage.builtInDesc: OpenWA will manage a MinIO (S3-compatible) container for you
storage.endpointHint: For MinIO or other S3-compatible services

restart.idleDesc:
Configuration has been saved to .env.generated.
A server restart is required to apply the changes.
restart.dontClose: Please do not close this window
restart.successMsg: Server is back online! The page will reload automatically.
restart.errorMsg: Server did not respond after 30 seconds. Please check the Docker logs and restart manually.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.subtitle: Manage engines, storage backends, and extensions

empty.title: No Plugins Found
empty.description: Install plugins to extend OpenWA functionality

config.title: Configure {{name}}
config.restartNotice: Changes require server restart to take effect
config.headlessDesc: Run browser without visible UI (recommended for production)
config.noOptions: No configuration options available for this plugin

toasts.savedDesc: Server restart required to apply changes.
